author: terencechain
content:
here is a prysm implementation which covers 625: https://github.com/OffchainLabs/prysm/pull/17124
it misses trusted bid and amount but follows the same shape, check out the config
feedback / question welcome 🙂

author: nflaig
content: so all the config related to builder basically is configured by the VC and the beacon node learn JIT (no static flag) which builders it needs to reach out to?

author: nflaig
content: I have that implemented in lodestar similar to that, it's easy if you pass a config file, but my biggest concern is how this will look like for setups that use the keymanager api, having a NxN (pubkey x builder) configuration map isn't entirely simple to express there, we could add a api that just dumps the raw file via post, mayne <@737740104281489531> has some thoughts, we should definitely keep that in mind, also others like charon, ssv etc. need to take a look at this. I don't worry at all that lodestar can implement all of this, I did this in a few hours already, but this change affects a lot of parties in the ecosystem and we need to get more feedback first

author: jvranek
content:
in commit-boost this is already somewhat of an issue that we have to deal with. when you have a node operator with 1000 keys that’s required to mux a subset of their keys to different relays, the layout in PR#17124 is awkward since you have to duplicate the BuilderEntry for each key. We invert it to have Relay -> keys and then provide simple batch key loading options. 

i can imagine something that would work in our use case and generally is if the VC could fetch the config map from an endpoint the different end users could supply, i.e., sidecars / DVT nodes etc

author: jvranek
content:
also just want to make sure I’m understanding this fully, Terence’s PR allows the VC to forward their preferences (from static config) to the BN, specifying the builder’s URL and optionally a `proxy` endpoint, `max_execution_payment`, and `pubkey.` The latter 2 fields allow the BN to ***validate*** a bid but there’s still not enough information for the BN to ***select*** the best bid. So Potuz’s is adding the `builder_boost_factor` and `min_bid` as additional fields to solve the bid selection problem. 

My gut is saying unless the builder should explicitly know about `builder_boost_factor` and `min_bid` (which I’m assuming they don’t), then instead of overloading the `BuilderPreferences` name (which the Builder API uses), to add another `BidPreferences` struct to house these fields. The VC’s config can then house `BuilderEntry` and `BidPreferences` and supply those to the BN.

author: terencechain
content: But for your mux case, you dont need the per key VC config at all, the per key layout and settings url exist for setup without filtering sidecar

author: terencechain
content: The bulder pub key section can just be empty and it should behave as a default to accept all
